headline: Survey at 35% completion with strong regional spread but significant scale readiness gap.
collection_status: 479 of 1,350 responses collected (35.5% progress). All 10 regions represented with Lindi, Mara, and Tanga leading (231 responses, 48% of current total). Pace suggests completion in 8-10 weeks if current momentum maintained.
key_findings: Teacher engagement strong: 323 responses (67%) from KiuFunza-trained teachers (lower + upper primary), indicating good reach within target cohort.
Expansion priority dominates: 175 stakeholders (37% of top priorities) want KiuFunza expanded; new programs (24%) and policy integration (20%) follow—signals demand exceeds current supply.
Critical readiness gap: 2.8/5 scale readiness score against 4.4/5 partner satisfaction suggests stakeholders support the initiative but lack confidence in implementation capacity.
Leadership underrepresented: Only 7 DEO, 5 ARC, 2 REO responses (14 total, 3% of survey) limits district/regional strategy insight; head teachers (115, 24%) partially compensate.
flags: Scale readiness (2.8/5) significantly lags satisfaction (4.4/5)—resource, training, or systems constraints blocking execution of expansion plans.
Regional imbalance: Lindi (89), Mara (78), Tanga (64) account for 48% of responses; Rukwa (17), Songwe (29), Singida (28) undersampled—risk of skewed insights.
Government/education official engagement critically low (14/479, 3%)—survey may miss policy feasibility constraints and won't validate ministerial buy-in for scale.
recommendation: Prioritize direct outreach to district and regional education officials (DEO, ARC, REO) in undersampled regions to close the 3% gap and validate scale readiness barriers before finalizing expansion strategy. Simultaneously investigate the 1.6-point satisfaction-readiness gap through targeted interviews with 20-30 stakeholders to isolate whether bottlenecks are funding, training, systems, or political commitment.
